clues: First described in 1859, this mucosa-limited disease was frequently fatal until the introduction of steroids in the 1950s.
Interestingly, smoking can mask milder forms of this disease or even be protective and non-smokers are at higher risk of developing this
Since this disease is restricted to the colon and rectum, surgical removal is considered a cure for bowel symptoms
Highly bioavailable, researchers are currently studying this drug for use in neurological disorders like autism and schizophrenia
Roughly 40-60 times more potent than others in this class of medications, this medication acts on the thick ascending limb
A nasal spray formulation, Embumyst, was approved by the FDA in 2025

For centuries, this was often mistaken for leprosy, causing patients to face extreme social stigma and isolation. We now know this condition is not contagious but rather is caused by cells multiplying up to ten times faster than normal
Ancient treatments included using snake venom, dog or cat feces, or arsenic, but UV light and biologic therapies are more effective
While this chronic autoimmune skin condition classically presents on knees and elbows, it can also affect the palms, soles, inside the mouth, and genital regions.
One of the oldest recorded medical conditions, this disease was described by Hippocrates as the 'unwalkable disease'
The name of this disease comes from Latin where medieval physicians believed the pain was caused by a drop of 'evil humors' seeping from the blood into affected areas

This is also called the 'disease of kings' due to the association with diets that only the wealthy could afford. Having this disease was seen as a mark of sophistication, raising the patient's social status.
Early signs of this disease often include asymmetric weakness in the finger flexors and quadriceps
Under the microscope, a muscle biopsy reveals both inflammation and degeneration leading to visible atrophy
Unlike other inflammatory myopathies, this progressive disease does not respond to immunosuppressive drugs or steroids
